entities.name: MICROPLASTICS
entities.description: Emerging global threat in all ecosystems with potential negative effects through multiple exposure pathways

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: Country where the study on used tyres and soil conservation was conducted

entities.name: USED TYRES
entities.description: Waste material repurposed to combat environmental pollution and used in agricultural plots

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL POLLUTION
entities.description: The contamination of the environment by waste materials like used tyres

entities.name: INFILTRATION
entities.description: The process by which water on the ground surface enters the soil

entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The draining away of water from the surface of an area of land

entities.name: MOISTURE
entities.description: The amount of water contained in the soil

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The removal of the top layer of soil, often by erosion

entities.name: PNEUSOL
entities.description: Agricultural technique involving gathering old tyres, cutting them into arcs, and positioning them in ridges to conserve soil and water

entities.name: EROSION CRUSTED SOIL
entities.description: Soil surface that has developed a hard crust due to erosion, used as the experimental site

entities.name: DJINDJILA
entities.description: Specific location in Mali where the experimental plots were installed

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing crops, which is stimulated by improved soil moisture and reduced runoff

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shift in temperatures and weather patterns causing harmful effects for farmers

entities.name: SOIL AMENDMENT
entities.description: The process of adding materials to soil to improve its properties

entities.name: DEGRADED LAND
entities.description: Land that has lost its biological or economic productivity

entities.name: CIRCULAR ECONOMY
entities.description: Economic system aimed at eliminating waste and the continual use of resources by repurposing waste into resources

relationships.description: reduces the amount of water runoff
reduces the amount of soil lost
utilizes used tyres as the primary material
serves as an effective agricultural production technique on
contributes to environmental pollution when not reused
repurposing them promotes a circular economy
combined with to make the technique more effective
